macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Fed Governor Waller warns inflation is still not moving in the right direction
items.summary: In a speech on the economic outlook, Governor Christopher Waller said the labor market appears stable and growth remains solid, but he stressed that inflation is still too high and broad-based. He pointed to elevated April CPI and PCE readings and said recent price data are “not good news.”

items.title: U.S. economic calendar points to a heavy data day, including GDP, personal income, and new home sales
items.summary: Trading Economics’ calendar shows several key U.S. releases and events, including second-estimate Q1 GDP, personal income, corporate profits, and new home sales, alongside Fed speeches. These are the main macro catalysts likely to shape market expectations for growth, inflation, and policy.

items.title: Conference Board maintains a modest 2026 U.S. growth outlook
items.summary: The Conference Board said it is currently projecting 1.7% year-over-year U.S. GDP growth in 2026, slightly higher than its prior 1.6% estimate. The update suggests a still-resilient but subdued macro backdrop.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI launches Deployment Company to help enterprises scale AI
items.summary: OpenAI has introduced a new enterprise-focused business aimed at helping organizations move AI systems from pilots into production. The effort centers on building, testing, and deploying AI across critical workflows.

items.title: EU lawmakers reach deal to delay parts of the AI Act
items.summary: EU countries and European Parliament lawmakers reportedly agreed to delay some high-risk AI compliance requirements, giving companies more time to prepare. Rules tied to AI-generated explicit content and watermarking are moving forward sooner.

items.title: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI over alleged medical chatbot claims
items.summary: Pennsylvania filed suit against Character Technologies, alleging that Character.AI chatbots misrepresented themselves as licensed medical professionals. The case highlights growing legal scrutiny of AI systems that may be perceived as offering health guidance.

items.title: Cerebras targets $3.5 billion IPO amid rising AI chip demand
items.summary: Cerebras is preparing a Nasdaq listing as investor interest in AI infrastructure and chips remains strong. The filing underscores continued momentum in the AI hardware market as demand for specialized compute accelerates.

items.title: House lawmakers seek more AI transparency from the SBA
items.summary: House Small Business Committee members advanced legislation that would require the Small Business Administration to report annually on its AI use. The move follows concerns raised by the GAO about how the agency is deploying AI tools.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie confirms Destiny 2 live-service updates are ending after June 9 patch
items.summary: Bungie has reportedly told players that the June 9 update will be the last major live-services update for Destiny 2, marking a major turning point for the long-running shooter. Reporting also indicates Bungie has no new project lined up for the Destiny 2 team and is shifting more resources toward Marathon.

items.title: Sony-owned Bungie faces reported layoffs as Destiny development winds down
items.summary: A Jason Schreier report cited in coverage says Sony's Bungie unit is planning significant layoffs as it ends development on Destiny 2. The report also says Bungie has not greenlit any new projects, including Destiny 3, and has been investing more in Marathon despite weaker-than-expected sales.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung unveils Galaxy Z TriFold with dual-hinge design and 10-inch display
items.summary: Samsung’s newest foldable introduces a redesigned dual-hinge mechanism and upgraded materials, pushing the company’s most advanced foldable device yet closer to mainstream consumer viability. The device unfolds from a pocketable form factor into a large 10-inch display.

items.title: Amazon brings Alexa+ to the web
items.summary: Amazon is expanding Alexa+ beyond dedicated devices and onto the web, signaling a broader push into agent-like digital assistants and direct competition with major consumer AI platforms. The move also extends Alexa’s reach into more everyday computing environments.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm push Android PCs toward the mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are working to bring Android PCs into the desktop market, aiming to challenge Microsoft and Apple by extending Android’s mobile dominance to personal computing. The effort could reshape the PC landscape if hardware and software support mature.

items.title: Quantum Motion deploys first full-stack quantum computer built with standard silicon fabrication
items.summary: Quantum Motion says it has deployed the first full-stack quantum computer made using standard silicon chip fabrication methods. If validated, the milestone could improve manufacturability and scalability for future quantum systems.

items.title: Qualcomm launches Snapdragon W5+ Gen 2 and W5 Gen 2 for wearables
items.summary: Qualcomm’s latest wearable chip platforms focus on improving battery life, connectivity, and design flexibility for smartwatches and other wearables. The update targets long-standing adoption barriers in the category.

items.title: AMD posts strong $9.2 billion quarter amid broader competitive pressure on Intel and Nvidia
items.summary: AMD’s latest quarterly results highlight disciplined execution and continued gains across enterprise and data center markets. The company is using performance and product strategy to challenge Intel while navigating constraints in the broader AI hardware supply chain.
